# Introduction 

The purpose of this notebook is to instruct the data operator on the operational steps in how to operate a dataflow. Other documentation such as development, database diagrams needs to be placed in other pages so it doesn't clutter the understanding of the work performed.  

We are currently in a design phase where we (together) will try to bring structure into all this. 

## WISE Helpdesk Support

**Helpdesk Portal (OTRS private system):** [OTRS](https://helpdesk.eionet.europa.eu/otrs/index.pl) 

- Internal Helpdesk FAQS 

**FAQs page for internal user (Taskman project):** [Wise-helpdesk](https://taskman.eionet.europa.eu/projects/wise-helpdesk) 


**Guidelines & rules** 


- Identify the tickets clearly in the subject once received I.e. [CountryCode - ReportingObligationTag- Subject of issue] 
- Expected Time Of Service is about 1-3 days (first analysis and manual reply) 
- For tickets opened by us in order to get feedback from MS - Set ticket to 'Pending reminder' and send 2 reminders before closing 

 

**Procedures**

- How to add a new reporter to a EIONET role (example): 

Sometimes countries may ask to add someone to the list of reporters. In this case, we'll just have to follow these guidelines: 
https://helpdesk.eionet.europa.eu/otrs/index.pl?Action=AgentFAQZoom;ItemID=39;Nav 

- Roles for WISE-SoE 
The WISE SoE helpdesk doesn't have permissions to add new national reporters to the respective lists. Please follow the instructions provided in the WISE SoE 2020 data call announcement (https://www.eionet.europa.eu/etcs/etc-icm/etc-news/etc-icm-data-call-wise-soe-data-call-2020): 

"Please contact your EIONET NFP if you require reporting permissions on a given dataflow, so that your user name is added to the authorised reporter list. Note that the WISE-4 reporters must be authorised as WISE-6 reporters to be able to report the water quality data, if it has not already been done." 

### Adding EIONET users to CDR roles 

The procedure to add an already existing EIONET user to a CDR role is pretty straight forward.

- First, we'll need to access http://www.eionet.europa.eu/ldap-roles 
- There, we'll search for the Role we're interested in, for example: 
- After following the link, on the top part of the page we'll see something like 
- We'll then be able to 'Add members' (for this, the user needs to exist beforehand) and then search for it and add it: 

## Data flows


### CDR Help

**Preamble**


Before 2015, the “Reporting guidelines” listed in ROD for the Water related data flows were pointing to a number of different locations in Forum, the ETC-ETC/ICM page, etc.. Following discussion with IDM2 (Søren Roug), the information was moved and is now kept in folders under http://cdr.eionet.europa.eu/help. Note that there is still old information in the ETC/ICM site, etc.: that information is to be moved as needed. 

**Who has permissions where?**



How to login? 
Management interface: Login at http://cdr.eionet.europa.eu/help/[YOUR FOLDER NAME]/manage using your EIONET account 

**How to set permissions?**
The owner of the LDAP group simply adds the EIONET user to the corresponding *-*-support group. 

See http://www.eionet.europa.eu/ldap-roles 

 

**How to add the entry page to a folder?**


**Special cases**

 

Each data flow under WISE_SoE has its own page. 

The http://cdr.eionet.europa.eu/help/WISE_SoE/wise5 folder is mantained by Tracasa and nerifern, who will keep it in sync with the WFD Spatial information. 

The http://cdr.eionet.europa.eu/help/WISE_SoE/FME_processes folder is mantained by Tracasa. 

The http://cdr.eionet.europa.eu/help/WISE_SoE/old directory contains old files (< 2014) that are now obsolete. 

### EIONET Roles for WISE 


How to see my own roles: https://projects.eionet.europa.eu/profile_overview?user= 

### contacts

## Meetings

### 2016-12 Second Data team meeting in CPH 
Meeting documents:  

Documents and presentations to be uploaded to the Forum meeting folder at: 

http://forum.eionet.europa.eu/etc-icm-consortium/library/subvention-2016/tasks-and-milestones-2016/1.5.5.-wise-development-technical-developments-integrated-management-data-flows/milestone-0-task-plan/2cd-data-team-meeting-december-2016  


### 2016-05-19 WISE registers and spatial data 
Agenda 

 

Part 1 

**Clarification of responsibilities:** 

  

- Data flow: WFD Spatial 

- Data processor: Bilbomatica/Guadaltel 

- Helpdesk support: Bilbomatica/Guadaltel 

- Geographic scope: EU-28 + NO (+ IS, if/when Iceland reports under WFD) 

  

- Data flow: WISE-5 

- Data processor: ETC-ICM 

- Helpdesk support: ETC-ICM 

- Geographic scope: EEA member countries (except EU-28 + NO + IS). EEA cooperating countries. Any country, if strictly under WISE-5 

  

**Responsibility for shared components:**

- Data model specification: EEA + ENV (meaning any changes are proposed and/or validated by EEA + ENV) 

- Data model implementation (schemas, templates,...): Bilbomatica/Guadaltel 

- QC specification: EEA + ENV (meaning any changes are proposed and/or validated by EEA + ENV) 

- QC implementation: Bilbomatica/Guadaltel 

- Automated harvesting from CDR to Working database: Bilbomatica/Guadaltel 

  

**Questions** 

(Alberto? Enrique? Olaf? meet before if need be, present ideas at the meeting): 

- Working spatial database specification (for the data reported in 2016 and after):  

        - what is implemented on the WFD side? 

        - how to duplicate/clone for WISE-5?  

- WISE reference spatial database: 

        - what is implemented on the WFD side? 

        - how to integrate the information coming from WISE-5? 

        - what is the relationship between the Working database and the reference database, and how does the latter become updated? 

- WISE registry: 

        - what is implemented on the WFD side for QC purposes? 

        - is it different from the WISE reference spatial database? 

        - how to extract the data so that it can be uploaded to the DD vocabularies? 

 

Part 2 

On-going tasks
